articles.title: Periscope Equity Invests in Amusement Connect, Signaling PE Interest in FEC Tech
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a technology platform serving the amusement and FEC industry. The deal highlights growing institutional investor confidence in FEC-focused software and operational tools. Operators should take note as PE-backed platforms often accelerate product development and expand support resources.

articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect
articles.summary: Periscope Equity has formally announced its investment in Amusement Connect, a management software platform used by amusement and entertainment venues. The backing could fuel new features, integrations, and wider adoption across the FEC sector. This is a notable signal that investors see scalable value in FEC operations technology.

articles.title: GrowthPro Group Launches Summer Education Series for FEC and Bowling Operators
articles.summary: The GrowthPro Group has announced a Summer 2026 lineup of Peer Talks and webinars tailored specifically for Family Entertainment Centers, bowling centers, and attractions operators. The series kicks off with a session on Artificial Intelligence in the Entertainment Industry. These low-cost educational touchpoints are a valuable resource for operators looking to stay competitive.

articles.title: FACE Amusement Group Opens 50,000-Sq-Ft Guy Fieri Entertainment Venue in Myrtle Beach
articles.summary: FACE Amusement Group has opened Guy Fieri's Downtown Flavortown at Myrtle Beach Mall, a 50,000-square-foot dining and entertainment destination developed with the celebrity chef. The venue follows a successful flagship in Pigeon Forge and demonstrates the ongoing strength of celebrity-branded FEC concepts in high-traffic tourist markets. The scale and co-branding strategy offer a useful model for operators considering anchor entertainment tenants.

articles.title: Attraktion! Celebrates Sold-Out Opening of Querion Hypermedia Entertainment Park in Poland
articles.summary: Querion Hypermedia Entertainment Park in Piechowice, Poland opened to a sold-out weekend, positioning itself as a year-round, all-weather indoor entertainment destination for families and groups. Developed by hotel operator Górskie Resorty with Attraktion!, the park blends hypermedia attractions with resort infrastructure. The strong debut underscores demand for indoor FEC-style destinations in Central European markets.

articles.title: SisyFox Launches Flexible Licensing for FoxBall Active Gaming Platform
articles.summary: SisyFox has released FoxBall 6.0 with new subscription and lifetime licensing options, lowering the barrier to entry for FEC operators interested in active digital gaming experiences. The subscription model reduces upfront investment while providing access to all software updates and new game content. This type of flexible pricing structure is increasingly important for smaller operators evaluating new attraction investments.

articles.title: Six Flags Expands Flexible Membership Program Across More Parks
articles.summary: Six Flags is rolling out its flexible membership program to additional park locations, reflecting a broader industry shift toward recurring revenue models in the attractions sector. Flexible memberships allow guests to pause or modify plans, reducing churn and increasing lifetime customer value. FEC operators of all sizes can draw lessons from how major chains are restructuring their loyalty and access offerings.

articles.title: Family Entertainment Thrives as Legoland Invests in Growth
articles.summary: Legoland is continuing to invest in expansion and new attractions, reinforcing the strength of the family entertainment segment in the current market. The brand's growth trajectory signals sustained consumer demand for immersive, family-friendly destinations. Independent FEC operators can benchmark against these investments as indicators of where the broader market is heading.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Expands Into Maryland, Continuing Nationwide Growth
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park is launching a new indoor entertainment location in Maryland as part of its continued national franchise expansion. The chain's steady rollout reflects strong franchisee demand and consumer appetite for large-format indoor adventure parks. Independent FEC operators in affected markets should be aware of increased competition from the growing brand.

articles.title: New Urban Air Adventure Park Opens in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park has opened a new location in Salem, Oregon, giving residents a first look at the chain's indoor trampoline and adventure park format. The opening represents another step in Urban Air's aggressive expansion across secondary U.S. markets. Local FEC operators should monitor how this affects regional foot traffic and consumer spending patterns.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Sees Global Boom
articles.summary: A new market report highlights booming worldwide growth in the location-based entertainment sector, driven by consumer demand for experiential outings and out-of-home entertainment. The report signals favorable macro conditions for FEC operators, suppliers, and investors looking to enter or expand in the space. Understanding these tailwinds can help operators make the case for capital investment and new concept development.

articles.title: Riot at Raleigh Urban Air Forces Early Closure, Raising Safety Concerns
articles.summary: Three women have been charged after a disturbance at an Urban Air location in Raleigh forced the family entertainment center to shut down early. The incident highlights the ongoing operational challenge of crowd management and security protocols at high-traffic FEC venues. Operators should review their incident response plans and staffing policies in light of similar events at peer venues.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Toy Foundation for International Day of Play, Offers Free Admission
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese is celebrating International Day of Play by offering free Adventure Zone admission and running a June fundraiser in partnership with the Toy Foundation. The promotion demonstrates how established FEC brands leverage cause-marketing to drive traffic and community goodwill. Operators can take note of this playbook for creating purpose-driven promotional events.

articles.title: Plymouth, UK to Get Major New Go-Kart and Laser Tag Entertainment Hub
articles.summary: A large new entertainment venue featuring go-karts and laser tag is set to open in Plymouth, England, adding a significant new competitor to the UK indoor FEC market. The project reflects continued investment in multi-attraction FEC formats across the UK. Suppliers and operators in the region should track this development as a gauge of market appetite for large-format venues.

articles.title: Multi-Million-Pound Entertainment Venue Opens at Brent Cross Shopping Centre, London
articles.summary: A new large-scale entertainment venue has opened at Brent Cross in London, adding to the growing trend of entertainment-anchored retail destinations in the UK. The opening illustrates how shopping centers continue to replace traditional retail with experience-driven FEC concepts. This model remains a key growth avenue for FEC operators seeking built-in foot traffic from mall environments.

articles.title: Tenpin Bowling and Entertainment Centre Opens in Blackpool
articles.summary: Tenpin has opened a new entertainment centre in Blackpool, expanding the UK chain's footprint in a key leisure destination market. The opening reflects Tenpin's ongoing investment in combining bowling with broader entertainment offerings to attract a wider demographic. UK FEC operators and investors should watch Tenpin's expansion strategy as a barometer for the sector.

articles.title: Madison, Mississippi to Break Ground on 'The Gallery' Entertainment District This Summer
articles.summary: A new entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison, Mississippi, adding a mixed-use FEC and dining destination to the market. Ground-breaking announcements like this are useful indicators of where developer capital is flowing in the regional FEC space. Suppliers and attraction vendors should monitor projects like this for early procurement opportunities.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Moves Forward at Jacksonville's Southside Commons
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is advancing development plans at the Southside Commons location in Jacksonville, Florida, signaling continued investor appetite for new FEC builds in the Southeast. The project adds to a pipeline of adventure park openings targeting suburban retail corridors. FEC suppliers and equipment vendors should flag this project as a near-term sales opportunity.

articles.title: Play Playground Nashville Introduces Monthly Cocktail Program to Drive Repeat Visits
articles.summary: Play Playground Nashville has launched a rotating monthly cocktail program called 'Playmaker's Pour,' debuting with patriotic-themed drinks for June and July. Beverage programs tied to seasonal themes are an increasingly effective tool for adult-oriented FEC venues to drive repeat visits and per-capita spending. Operators running social gaming or entertainment bar concepts should consider similar programming to boost F&B revenue.

articles.title: Brogent Technologies Opens New R&D and Manufacturing Facility in Taiwan
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies has inaugurated a new facility at Ciaotou Science Park in Kaohsiung, Taiwan, expanding its capacity for research, development, manufacturing, and system integration of immersive ride systems. The investment strengthens Brogent's ability to deliver media-based attractions globally, including to FEC and LBE operators. Operators evaluating media-based ride systems should expect improved lead times and product development from this supplier.

articles.title: Brogent Unveils 360-Degree R-Ride at IAAPA Expo Asia, Announces New IP Partnership
articles.summary: Brogent Technologies debuted its new R-Ride at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026, featuring a 360-degree panoramic screen, motion seating, and full rotation system designed to deliver immersive, high-intensity experiences. The company also announced a partnership with a major Japanese IP holder, signaling expanded content options for attraction buyers. FEC operators evaluating media-based rides should watch for R-Ride's commercial availability following its VinWonders debut in late 2027.

articles.title: London-Originated Social Gaming Venue with Foosball and Virtual Soccer Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A London-based indoor entertainment concept featuring foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is set to open in Paris, expanding the social gaming FEC model into a major European capital. The venue format mirrors the growing trend of blending competitive socializing with food and beverage in an FEC setting. European FEC operators should take note of this concept's international expansion as a potential competitor and format benchmark.

articles.title: Bear River Casino Resort Unveils New Entertainment Zone for Local Community
articles.summary: Chamber members from Fortuna and Eureka toured the new entertainment zone at Bear River Casino Resort, highlighting the venue's appeal as a local leisure destination beyond its gaming floor. The project illustrates how hospitality and gaming venues are integrating FEC-style entertainment to attract broader demographics. Independent FEC operators in mixed hospitality markets should monitor how casino-adjacent entertainment competes for the same leisure dollar.

articles.title: Dream Garden Emphasizes Localization and Trust in Global Indoor Playground Projects
articles.summary: Indoor playground manufacturer Dream Garden has published insights on the importance of localization and trust-building in international project delivery. The piece addresses how playground equipment suppliers can tailor designs and operations to regional cultural and regulatory requirements. FEC operators planning international expansion or sourcing equipment globally will find practical perspective in this vendor's approach.

articles.title: Popular Essex Social Golf Venue Closes, Highlighting Risks in Experiential Leisure Sector
articles.summary: A well-known social golf course in Essex, UK, described as a 'social playground,' has shut its doors, serving as a cautionary note for operators in the competitive experiential leisure space. The closure underscores that even popular, trendy FEC formats are not immune to financial or operational challenges. Operators in the social gaming and mini-golf segment should review their unit economics and lease structures in light of this development.

articles.title: Sabre Lanes in Menasha Permanently Closes, Directing Bowlers to Appleton Location
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes, a longstanding bowling center in Menasha, Wisconsin, has permanently closed and is directing its customer base to a nearby Appleton facility. The closure continues a trend of independent bowling center consolidation across the Midwest. Operators in the bowling segment should take note of the competitive dynamics as customers in smaller markets increasingly funnel toward larger, better-equipped venues.
